compressed_prompt: Write two limericks for moms about how hard it is to get their kids to do chores. Be angry about it. Separate your two limericks with six asterisks (******).
response:
There once was a mom full of cheer,
Whose kids wouldn’t lend an ear.
“Just take out the trash!”
“No, I’d rather just dash!”
Her patience was gone—oh, so near!

******

A mother would plead, shout, and beg,
“Just clean up your mess—it’s one leg!”
But the kids just would grin,
Leave the chaos within,
And she’d scream, “I’m this close to the edge!”
